title: FHA Loan Limits Florida 2026 | County-by-County FHA Mortgage Limits
description: 2026 FHA loan limits for all 67 Florida counties, with 1-to-4-unit amounts. The floor is $541,287, up to $990,150 in Monroe County (the Keys). Sourced from HUD.

bodyHtml:
Home/FHA Loan Limits Florida
FHA Loan Limits Florida

FHA loan limits in Florida (2026)

The 2026 FHA loan and mortgage limits in Florida range from a statewide floor of $541,287 to $990,150 in Monroe County. Here's the full county-by-county breakdown by property type.

Get My Free Rate QuoteCall __PHONE_DISPLAY__
Floor: $541,287Monroe: $990,150Updated for 2026All 67 counties

Get your free FHA quote

No obligation. See your rate & payment in minutes.

🔒 Your information is secure. By submitting you agree to be contacted about FHA loan options. This is not a commitment to lend.

Thank you!

An FHA mortgage specialist will reach out shortly with your personalized Florida rate quote.

2026 Loan Limits

Florida FHA loan limits by county for 2026

FHA loan limits cap the maximum mortgage the Federal Housing Administration will insure — and they vary by county based on local home prices. For 2026, most Florida counties sit at the national floor of $541,287 for a single-family home, rising to $990,150 in Monroe County (the Florida Keys), the highest in the state. These limits apply to FHA case numbers assigned on or after January 1, 2026.

Below are the 2026 limits for all 67 Florida counties, for 1- to 4-unit properties. One key point: the limit caps the loan amount, not the purchase price — you can still buy a pricier home if you put enough down to bring the loan under your county's cap.

State floor (1-unit)$541,287

Most Florida counties

State high (Monroe)$990,150

At A Glance

The six 2026 limit tiers

Florida's counties fall into six limit tiers for 2026. Most are at the floor; a handful of higher-cost metros are above it:

Source & verification: Figures reflect HUD's published 2026 FHA loan limits. Multi-unit amounts are rounded and limits can be revised, so always confirm the exact figure for your county and unit count using HUD's official FHA Mortgage Limits lookup tool before relying on it. This page is informational and not a commitment to lend.

The Formula

How HUD sets each county's limit

FHA limits are tied by law to the conforming loan limit set each year by the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA). For 2026 the baseline conforming limit is $832,750, and FHA applies two formulas to it:

  • The floor is 65% of the conforming limit — $541,287 for 2026 — and applies to lower-cost counties (most of Florida).
  • The ceiling is 150% of the conforming limit — $1,249,125 — and applies to the most expensive metros nationwide.
  • In between, a county's limit is set at 115% of its area median home price, rounded to the nearest $50.
  • Limits are set metro-wide: the highest-priced county in a metro area sets the limit for the whole metro, which is why all of South Florida's tri-county area shares the $667,000 figure.
  • Multi-unit limits are fixed multiples of the 1-unit figure — roughly 1.28× for a duplex, 1.55× for a triplex, and 1.92× for a fourplex.
Over The Limit

If your home is priced above the cap

What if my home costs more than the FHA limit?

Remember the limit caps your loan amount, not the price. If your target home pushes past the county cap, you have options:

  • Put more down. A larger down payment shrinks the loan under the limit — e.g., in a $541,287-limit county, a $600,000 home with $60,000 down leaves a $540,000 loan that still fits FHA.
  • Go conventional. The 2026 conforming limit is $832,750 in most of Florida — far above the FHA floor — so a conforming loan may give you more room if your credit and down payment qualify.
  • Use a jumbo loan for amounts above the conforming limit.
  • Consider an FHA 203(k) if the home needs work — it bases the loan on the after-renovation value within the same county limit.
Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Florida for 2026?+
For a single-family home, 2026 FHA loan limits in Florida range from $541,287 in most counties up to $990,150 in Monroe County (the Florida Keys). Limits are higher for 2-, 3-, and 4-unit properties.
Which Florida county has the highest FHA loan limit?+
Monroe County, home to the Florida Keys and Key West, has the state’s highest 2026 FHA limit at $990,150 for a 1-unit home, up to about $1.9 million for a fourplex.
What are the FHA limits in Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach?+
All three South Florida counties share a 2026 one-unit limit of $667,000, with higher amounts for multi-unit properties.
Do FHA loan limits depend on the home price or the loan amount?+
The loan amount. The limit caps how much FHA will insure, not what you can pay for the home. If you put enough down to keep the loan under your county’s limit, you can still buy a more expensive house with FHA.
When do the 2026 FHA loan limits take effect?+
They apply to FHA loans with a case number assigned on or after January 1, 2026. Your lender requests the case number once you’re under contract.
Are FHA limits higher for a duplex or fourplex?+
Yes. Multi-unit limits are higher than 1-unit limits. In most Florida counties the 2026 figures are $693,050 for a duplex, $837,700 for a triplex, and $1,041,125 for a fourplex, and they rise further in high-cost counties.
Where can I confirm my county’s exact FHA limit?+
Use HUD’s official FHA Mortgage Limits lookup tool at entp.hud.gov, or ask your loan officer. The table above reflects HUD’s 2026 figures, but it’s always worth confirming the exact number for your county and property type.
